caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) for individuals over age 72. The page explains that RMDs are taxable income and can incur penalties if not taken on time. It specifies that traditional IRA, SEP, SARSEP, and SIMPLE IRA account holders must start taking distributions by age 72, even if they are still working. For those reaching age 72 in 2022, the first RMD is due by April 1, 2023, and subsequent RMDs by December 31 each year thereafter. The video also covers retirement plans like 401(k), 403(b), and 457(b), stating that the first RMD is due by April 1 of the later of the year they reach age 72 or retire. A 5% owner of the employer must begin RMDs at age 72. The cursor moves through the text, highlighting key points as it goes.
